---
title: "Pythia"
page-layout: full
title-block: false
---

```{=html}
<nav class="page-nav">
  <a class="page-nav-brand" href="#top">Pythia</a>
  <div class="page-nav-links">
    <a href="#project-details">Project details</a>
    <a href="#video-demo">Video demo</a>
    <a href="#team">Team</a>
    <a href="#awards">Awards</a>
  </div>
  <div class="page-nav-lang">
    <a href="./">EN</a>
    <a href="../es/">ES</a>
  </div>
</nav>
```

::: {#top .hero-shell}
[]{.eyebrow} Technical case study with product polish

# Pythia { .hero-title }

::: {.hero-subtitle}
Data Science for Cafeterias

Final project for the Master in Data Science at UCM/NTIC.
:::

::: {.badge-row}
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Real world project</strong>
<span>Uses real sales and customer review data from a Madrid coffee shop to build a practical, intelligent cafeteria application.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Integrates distinct fields of knowledge</strong>
<span>Effectively combines coursework in ETL, EDA, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Time Series, NLP, and advanced visualization into one coherent solution.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Built as a real product</strong>
<span>Presented as a functioning MVP in Streamlit, showing the project as a usable product rather than only an academic deliverable.</span>
:::
::: {.hero-badge}
<strong>Recognitions</strong>
<span>Received the maximum grade and earned 2nd place in a pitch contest.</span>
:::
:::

::: {.cta-row}
[Watch Demo](#video-demo){.cta-button .cta-tertiary}
:::
:::

## Project Details {#project-details .section-title}

Pythia is designed to be readable in one sitting: start with the business challenge, move through the analytical pipeline, review the modeling choices, and finish with measurable impact.

::: {.detail-grid}
::: {.content-card .detail-card .simple-card}
### Business Problem

Specialty cafes operate with tight margins, perishable products, and highly variable demand. This project addresses that challenge by combining sales forecasting and customer review analysis to support better decisions on purchasing, staffing, and business improvement priorities.
:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Data and Methodology

The project combines structured and unstructured data, including daily sales records, weather data, holiday calendars, CaixaForum visitor flows, and Google reviews. Using a CRISP-DM approach, it integrates time-series forecasting and text mining into a single decision-support system for the cafe.

:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Modeling

For forecasting, Prophet was selected after comparison with Linear Regression and XGBoost, offering the best balance between accuracy, robustness, and interpretability. For text mining, the pipeline combines YAKE, NLTK, and Sentence-BERT to automatically extract, normalize, and group customer feedback themes from reviews.

:::

::: {.content-card .detail-card}
### Results and Impact

The forecasting models achieved strong results, with revenue prediction reaching about 11.74% MAPE in validation and 14.25% in production monitoring. The review analysis identified key strengths such as food, coffee, and service, while highlighting price as the main improvement area. Both components were deployed in a Streamlit app, turning the project into a practical tool for real business decision-making.

:::
:::

## Video Demo {#video-demo .section-title}

::: {.youtube-frame}
<iframe src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/gkD9bKw1ikY" title="Pythia demo video" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
:::

## Team {#team .section-title}

Pythia was shaped by a collaborative team with a shared focus on technical quality, product thinking, and clear communication.

::: {.team-grid}
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anabel-baez-996348a4/){.team-link}

### Anabel Baez Rodriguez
:::
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fatimatawfikvazquez/){.team-link}

### Fatima Tawfik Vazquez
:::
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/genesishernandezg/){.team-link}

### Genesis Hernandez Gallegos
:::
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilan-arvelo-yagua/){.team-link}

### Ilan Arvelo Yagua
:::
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luca-iacomino-58440b180/){.team-link}

### Luca Iacomino
:::
::: {.team-card}
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yassuhiro-m/){.team-link}

### Yassuhiro Marcio
:::
:::

## Awards {#awards .section-title}

The project combined strong technical work with strong communication, and the outcomes reflect both.

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.award-card}
### Maximum grade
Pythia received the highest academic evaluation for the final project.
:::
::: {.award-card}
### 2nd place in pitch contest
The project also stood out in a presentation setting, validating its clarity, storytelling, and practical positioning.

[View LinkedIn post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ganadores-competici%C3%B3n-becas-ds-ntic-master-ugcPost-7434270925170323456-QIfv?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAFKEmJ4BXrPRzmBCAnGrHF7nwaxBzRCsCh0)
:::
:::

## Contact {#contact .section-title}

For opportunities, technical discussion, or a walkthrough of the project, reach out through the channels below.

::: {.contact-grid}
::: {.contact-card}
### LinkedIn
[linkedin.com/in/yassuhiro-m](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yassuhiro-m/)
:::
::: {.contact-card}
### Pythia Project Repo
[github.com/lucaiaco21/Cafe-Madrid---TFM-Project](https://github.com/lucaiaco21/Cafe-Madrid---TFM-Project)
:::
::: {.contact-card}
### GitHub
[github.com/YassuhiroM](https://github.com/YassuhiroM)
:::
:::

Pythia  
Master’s Final Project at UCM  
Data Science  
Available for technical interviews, project walkthroughs, and collaboration conversations
